notes: MB embedded_mbid confirms title/year 1964/Kapp Records/Louis Armstrong. albumDNA matches the Wikipedia (Louis Armstrong album) extract verbatim; sidemen, recording dates, Billboard chart-topper all consistent. All 6 keyTracks present in MB tracklist.

MB embedded_mbid confirms exact title/1964/Argo ZDA 26 and the full artist credit (Adrian Mitchell, Dannie Abse, The Michael Garrick Quintet, Joe Harriott, Shake Keane) — every DNA fact matches. Stored wiki URL is the Joe Harriott artist page (correct artist, not the album); ours.wikipediaUrl is null so none_stored. Both 'Poetry and Jazz' keyTracks match MB.

MB embedded_mbid: title/1964-06/Blue Note BLP 4164/Jimmy Smith (+Stanley Turrentine). DNA 'recorded in 1963... rereleased on CD with two bonus tracks recorded at a separate session in 1960' is supported by the wiki NOTE ('Lonesome Road and Smith Walk... recorded on June 13, 1960'). Wiki main text says 'from the same session' but its own NOTE confirms the 1960 separate session, so DNA is accurate. All 5 keyTracks match MB. Stored wiki URL resolves to the correct album page.

MB search (similarity 1.0) confirms title/Blue Note BLP 4230/Hank Mobley and all 4 keyTracks. The only discrepancy is the year: ours 1965 vs MB release date and release-group first-release date both 1966. Likely a recorded-late-1965 / released-1966 ambiguity — low-stakes — but the MB evidence does contradict 1965. Sidemen Lee Morgan/Curtis Fuller are editorial (uncheckable, uncontradicted).

Entity solidly confirmed: MB embedded_mbid title/1965-06/Argo (Argo 749)/Baby-Face Willette, all 5 keyTracks match. Substantive claims that cannot be checked against the evidence: 'his final album', 'recorded in late 1964', 'Cut in Chicago'. No contradiction, but these are uncheckable. The stored evidence wiki URL is 'Face to Face (Baby Face Willette album)' — a DIFFERENT album (1961 Blue Note debut), wrong entity; ours.wikipediaUrl is null so none_stored. The DNA's reference to earlier Blue Note sessions is consistent with that page.

MB search (1.0): title/1965-06-18/Blue Note BLP 4209/Hank Mobley. All 4 keyTracks present in MB tracklist, and 'The Dip' is indeed track 1 ('the opener'). Remainder is editorial opinion plus sideman naming (Lee Morgan, Harold Mabern Jr.) — legitimate editorial style per spec. No contradictions.

MB embedded_mbid confirms every DNA fact: title/1965/Verve V6-8615/co-led by Ray Brown and Milt Jackson, and all 5 keyTracks. The stored evidence wiki URL is 'Goodbye (Milt Jackson album)' (1973 CTI) — wrong entity — but ours.wikipediaUrl is null so none_stored.

MB search (1.0): title/1965/Verve/Cal Tjader. All 4 keyTracks match, including the title track 'Soul Sauce (Guachi Guaro)'. Prose is editorial opinion (biggest commercial success, most popular Latin jazz artist). The one factual attribution — Dizzy Gillespie-penned title track — is uncheckable in the evidence but uncontradicted (and the track title 'Guachi Guaro' is consistent). Opensearch wiki resolved to Mexican Institute of Sound / 'Soy Sauce' — wrong entity, discarded; ours.wikipediaUrl null so none_stored.

MB embedded_mbid: title/1966/MPS/Willie “The Lion” Smith; all 5 keyTracks match. The bio sentence matches the Willie 'the Lion' Smith wiki page verbatim (correct artist). CAVEAT: DNA says the album is 'documenting the breadth of the free jazz period' — Smith is a stride pianist and this is a 1966 stride record, so the free-jazz era framing is wrong/templated noise. Not flagged as factual_error because no evidence passage verbatim contradicts the album's genre (absence is not contradiction); a repair-writer should drop the free-jazz framing. Stored wiki URL is the artist page; ours.wikipediaUrl null so none_stored.

MB search (1.0): title/1966/ESP-Disk/Albert Ayler (+Cherry, Tchicai, Rudd, Peacock, Murray); releaseGroup secondaryType Soundtrack. DNA matches the wiki extract verbatim (recorded July 1964, soundtrack for Michael Snow's film). All 3 keyTracks match MB. Wiki short-desc says '1965 album' but ours' 1966 matches the MB release date (recorded 1964, released 1966). Stored wiki URL correct.

Entity confirmed: MB embedded_mbid title/1966/Atlantic 1460/Nat Adderley, all 6 keyTracks match. Substantive uncheckable claim: the album's structure ('four performances by Adderley with an 11-piece orchestra and four by Adderley's Quintet with Joe Henderson, Herbie Hancock, Bob Cranshaw, and Roy McCurdy') is not in the evidence — no contradiction but cannot be verified. Also the 'free jazz era ... abandoned predetermined structures' framing conflicts internally with the 'bebop conversation' line and is templated noise (not a citable factual error). Opensearch wiki returned nothing.

MB embedded_mbid: title/1967-11/Capitol Records/Ella Fitzgerald; all 6 keyTracks match the MB tracklist (MB lists '...- Remastered 2006' suffixes; 'Away in the Manger' vs MB 'Away in a Manger' is a trivial article variant of the same carol). The bio sentence matches the Ella Fitzgerald wiki page closely (ours adds 'absolute pitch', not in the cited extract — absence, not contradiction). CAVEAT: 'draws from the free jazz tradition' is plainly wrong for a 1967 album of Christmas carols — templated era noise; not a citable factual_error but a repair-writer should remove it. Stored wiki URL is the artist page; ours.wikipediaUrl null so none_stored.

MB embedded_mbid: title/1967/RCA Victor LPV-546/Jelly Roll Morton; all 6 keyTracks match. This is a 1967 RCA Victor reissue/compilation of Morton's 1920s-30s recordings (he died 1941). CAVEAT: 'delivers a free jazz program ... from the free jazz era' is wrong (Morton is early/New Orleans jazz) and frames a long-dead artist as making a 1967 program — templated noise; not a citable factual_error (no contradicting evidence quote). Opensearch wiki returned nothing.

Entity confirmed: MB embedded_mbid title/Blue Note/Dexter Gordon; releaseGroup firstReleaseDate 1967 matches ours' year (the MB release itself is a 2004 reissue). All 3 keyTracks match. Substantive uncheckable claim: 'recorded on June 2, 1964, in Paris' is not in the evidence (no contradiction, cannot verify). Personnel (Byrd, Drew, NHOP, Art Taylor) is sideman naming. Opensearch wiki returned nothing.

MB search (1.0): title/1967/impulse! AS-9138/Pharoah Sanders. DNA facts (second album, first for Impulse!, recorded Nov 1966 while playing with Coltrane, released 1967) all confirmed by the entity-matched Tauhid (album) wiki page. keyTracks 'Aum' and 'Venus' match as substrings of the MB medley 'Aum / Venus / Capricorn Rising'; all 4 matchable. Stored wiki URL correct.

MB embedded_mbid confirms every claim: title/1968-10/RCA Red Seal LSC-3052 and the full artist credit (Weber as composer, Benny Goodman, Chicago Symphony, Jean Martinon). All 6 movement keyTracks match the MB tracklist exactly. Opensearch wiki returned nothing; ours.wikipediaUrl null so none_stored.

No MB match (release null, empty searchCandidates). The stored evidence wiki page is 'Grazing in the Grass' — the 1968 COMPOSITION/single page, about Hugh Masekela's recording, not the album under verification. It corroborates the artist (Hugh Masekela), the title track 'Grazing in the Grass', and 1968 — enough to confirm the right entity — but it does NOT confirm the album title 'Grazin'/Hugh Masekela's Grazin’', the Uni label, or the other keyTracks (Bajabula Bonke, etc.). Those album-level facts are unverifiable. No MB tracklist so keyTracks no_evidence. ours.wikipediaUrl null so none_stored.

MB search (1.0): title/1968/Solid State Records SS 18039/Chick Corea (MB release is a Greek pressing). DNA matches the entity-matched wiki extract verbatim (second studio album, Dec 1968, Solid State, trio with Vitous and Haynes, 1988 Blue Note CD reissue with eight bonus tracks). All 4 keyTracks match MB. Stored wiki URL correct.

MB embedded_mbid: title/1968/Columbia CS 9655/Art Tatum; all 5 keyTracks match. The closing sentence matches the Art Tatum wiki page verbatim (correct artist). CAVEAT: 'delivers a swing program ... from the big band era' frames a 1968 Columbia reissue (compiling Tatum's 1933 solo sides and 1949 concert recordings; Tatum died 1956) as a contemporary release — templated era noise; not a citable factual_error. Stored wiki URL is the artist page; ours.wikipediaUrl null so none_stored.

Entity unambiguous: MB embedded_mbid (releaseGroup 'Selflessness, Featuring My Favorite Things', impulse!, John Coltrane); DNA matches the entity-matched wiki extract nearly verbatim (posthumous, juxtaposes 1963 Newport live tracks with the 1965 LA studio 'Selflessness'). keyTracks match MB tracklist. TWO evidence-internal discrepancies to note (neither a citable error against ours): (1) year field 1968 matches MB releaseGroup firstReleaseDate 1968, while the DNA text and wiki both say 'released in 1969'; (2) keyTrack 'I Want to Know About You' matches the MB tracklist exactly, but the wiki track listing names it 'I Want to Talk About You (Billy Eckstine)' — MB itself appears to carry a 'Know'/'Talk' typo. Since ours matches MB, keyTracks ok; flag for a human.

MB embedded_mbid confirms every DNA fact: title/1968/Pickwick/33 Records SPC-3187/Nat King Cole. All 5 keyTracks match the MB tracklist; the three tracks named in the prose (April in Paris, My Heart Stood Still, I Want to Be Happy) are present. Stored wiki URL is the Nat King Cole artist page (correct artist); ours.wikipediaUrl null so none_stored.

MB search (1.0): title/1969/Vortex Records/Sonny Sharrock. DNA matches the entity-matched Black Woman (album) wiki extract verbatim (debut, recorded 1969, Vortex, 2000 Collectables reissue paired with People Get Ready). All 4 keyTracks match MB. 'Invented free jazz guitar' / punk-noise-metal influence lines are editorial opinion. Stored wiki URL correct.

MB search (1.0) confirms title/Delmark Records DS-420/421/Anthony Braxton, and all 4 keyTracks (dedications) match. The dedications-to-different-musicians claim is confirmed by the tracklist; 'first full-length solo saxophone recording in jazz history' is an editorial/historical claim (uncheckable, uncontradicted). Discrepancy: ours year 1969 vs MB release date and release-group first-release date both 1971 (recorded ~1969, released 1971). The opensearch wiki resolved to 'For altid (album)' by Danish electropop singer Medina — wrong entity, discarded; ours.wikipediaUrl null so none_stored.

MB embedded_mbid: releaseGroup 'Monk's Blues' firstReleaseDate 1969/CBS/Thelonious Monk (the MB release itself is a 1990 reissue). ours' year 1969 matches the release-group first-release date. All 5 keyTracks match MB, and 'Straight No Chaser' named in the prose is also present. 'large-ensemble setting' is consistent and uncontradicted. The stored evidence wiki URL is 'Monk's Dream' (1963) — a DIFFERENT album, wrong entity — but ours.wikipediaUrl is null so none_stored.

MB search (1.0): title/1969/Flying Dutchman FD-10115/Leon Thomas. All 4 keyTracks match the MB tracklist. The prose is heavily editorial (yodeling technique, pygmy-yodeling vocabulary, bridges African/free-jazz/soul) plus sideman naming (Pharoah Sanders) — legitimate editorial style; the 'debut' claim is uncheckable but uncontradicted. Opensearch wiki returned nothing.
